channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Grass verge not cut, 5th June
item.description:
Please can someone at Bromley council explain why you have decided to stop cutting the grass verge on Worsley Bridge Road between Montana Gardens and Meadowview Road? It lies within the Bromley council area and it's even under the "London borough of Bromley" sign! I've had to repeatedly report this missed grass verge since about 2019!!!!!!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Worsley Bridge Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missed grass cut, 5th June
item.description:
We live in Sellindge Close Beckenham BR31PQ and the grass cutters have missed the 2 pieces of grass. They cut cooers cope road but as usual missed these 2 pieces of grassin Sellindge Close BR31PQ. Also the bush on 1 piece of the grass is covering the path and can't see if cars are driving on the road which is extremely dangerous as there's alot of young children here. Please can you send a grass cutting crew to sellinge Close BR31PQ to cut the grass and trim bush

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sellindge Close, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter in kurbside needs removing please, 5th June
item.description:
.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mottingham Lane, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of litter in street, 5th June
item.description:
There is lots of litter on the street and pavement on Vestry Road and Oswyth Road. Can you please ask the street cleaning team to do a pass off the area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Vestry Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Four Grey bags a mirror and some pieces of wood, 5th June
item.description:
Dumped outside 253 Cray High St

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 253, High Street, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous Tree ,, 5th June
item.description:
This is not a Pot Hole problem, i am reporting on what i feel is a risk to the Residents and general public, The Tree in question is in the garden of No 24 Drummond Close, This estate is under a Housing Association of Places for people, who then employ their subsidiary firm RMG Living to Manage the site, Because the tree , a fir tree, has grown so big it it has a massive sail area, and i fear the next big storm could bring it crashing down, its only four feet from the pavement, see attached photo, i have now twice sent an email to RMG Property manager who is responsible for such issues , and to date i have not received a reply, so i must now ask the council to approach RMG Living and inquire if they are going to carry out a risk assessment of this tree, i live opposite this tree and i regard i am one of those who are at risk of serious injury if this tree comes down, The name of the Property Manager at RMG is Aleshia Scott, Aleshia‑Marie Scott ​​​​ Property Manager T: 03450024444 E: AleshiaMarie.Scott@rmguk.com Residential Management Group Ltd As said, i have sent them RMG a photo and voiced my concerns, but they have ignored both of my emails , i know this tree is not rooted in original soil, because the house were built on top of a Rubbish tip , and the topsoil is a mixure of builders rubble, and when you consider the roots of Douglas firs only go down 2 or 3 ft, and being this tree is only 4 ft from the side of the house, i doubt if the toots soread in that direction,

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Drummond Close, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked on pavement, 5th June

Parked on pavement obstructing mother with strollers and disabled people

item.title: Bins left on pavement after collection, 5th June
item.description:
Bin collectors leaving food waste bins on the public pathway My daughter tripped on the bin and is now unable to walk Bins should be placed back onto the property not left on public footpath
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eden Way, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kerb stone moved. Trip Hazard., 5th June
item.description:
Kerb stones moved AGAIN. This is a repeated issue along this section with several kerb stones moved, likely caused by vans driving/parking on the pavement. Not helped by them not being fixed in place properly last time. Now a dangerous trip hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 148, Bermondsey Wall East, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flyposters removed, 5th June
item.description:

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Weeds O/S 23, 25 and 27 Mayfair Avenue, 5th June
item.description:
I would like to report a section of pavement that has become overgrown with weeds. The weeds are growing through cracks and along the edges of the pavement, making the area look neglected. I would be grateful if the area could be inspected and the weeds removed as part of the council's maintenance programme. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mayfair Avenue, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish on pavement, 5th June
item.description:
Rubbish left by bin men all along the pavement in Lennard Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Lennard Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repair to wooden rail, 5th June
item.description:
Missing wooden rail by the Church House Gardens pond and amphitheatre has been made safe, but it would be good if it could have a proper rail replacement like the great repair recently carried out along the zig zag path up to the Churchill theatre.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tetty Way, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hedge blocking pavement, 5th June
item.description:
An unkept hedge bush is blocking access on the pavement. This is causing obstruction for wheelchair users and prams, who have to walk in the road instead.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gylcote Close, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
